objective: Implement the first optimization-focused fork-only release on top of current functional baseline: sanitize OpenAI/OpenRouter response contracts, harden stream parsing, and reduce user-facing error leakage while preserving raw SSE opt-in.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible fork-local updates across src/engine.py, src/utils.py, src/handler.py, .runpod/hub.json, README.md, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: When `RAW_OPENAI_OUTPUT=false`, OpenAI non-stream responses are normalized to stable JSON objects with expected top-level and per-choice key presence where possible.
When `RAW_OPENAI_OUTPUT=false`, OpenAI stream parsing accepts only SSE `data:` lines, ignores control/comment lines, and preserves `[DONE]` stream termination semantics.
Malformed streamed chunks do not crash parsing; worker emits structured error payload and logs detailed trace server-side.
User-facing error payloads avoid raw exception internals; detailed exception traces remain in logs.
RunPod hub default for `RAW_OPENAI_OUTPUT` is `false`, while `RAW_OPENAI_OUTPUT=true` keeps raw SSE passthrough behavior.
README and runtime compatibility docs describe exact OpenAI/OpenRouter structured mode behavior and raw mode opt-in.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this release to restore previous OpenAI payload behavior and `RAW_OPENAI_OUTPUT=true` hub default. Operational mitigation without code rollback: set `RAW_OPENAI_OUTPUT=true` on endpoint config to force raw SSE passthrough while investigating parser/sanitization regressions. Re-run full validation gate before redeploy.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). Validate OpenAI non-stream and stream requests (including malformed stream simulation) at each stage before scaling.
threat_model: Main risks are (1) client-side assumptions about previous raw chunk structure in structured mode and (2) malformed upstream chunk handling causing unexpected structured errors. Mitigations: keep raw passthrough opt-in unchanged, keep changes minimal and centralized in utils helpers, log full stack traces server-side, and roll out 1->2->4 with fast rollback via env toggle or revert.
